claim: Leaderboard rankings across different sources (Arena, Design Arena, Artificial Analysis) often disagree significantly on the same models
actionable: Evaluate models on multiple independent leaderboards; don't rely on a single ranking to guide model selection
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: Most language models lose at least 40% of head-to-head battles in leaderboard evaluations, meaning the top model is wrong for nearly half of use cases
actionable: Use Pareto frontiers (quality vs. latency or cost) instead of single rankings; expect 3-4 models clustered tightly in quality but varying 20x in efficiency
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: Evaluating a single image generation model on 26,000 samples takes 20 days of compute, costs $5,000, and uses 400 marathons of energy
actionable: Optimize evaluations: use efficient models, compress evaluations, or reduce sample counts proportionally; measure tokens per successful outcome, not just quality
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: The same evaluation using compressed/efficient models takes 7 hours, costs $265, and uses 4 marathons worth of energy
actionable: Invest in model compression (quantization, pruning, distillation) to achieve 20-30x compute savings while maintaining usable quality
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: Manual inspection and subjective preference are unreliable for model evaluation; multiple humans and clear evaluation metrics are needed
actionable: Never rely on manual inspection alone; use human evaluation at scale with multiple judges and predefined scoring rubrics
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: Metric variations across datasets can be huge; CLIP score rankings change significantly when applied to different image datasets
actionable: Always use task-specific metrics aligned with your use case, not generic aggregated metrics; validate on representative data
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna

claim: Model compression via quantization, pruning, and step reduction (e.g., 50 steps to 4) can achieve massive speedup (20x+) with minimal quality loss
actionable: Apply per-module quantization, structured pruning, and denoiser step caching for diffusion models to compress while preserving quality
source_title: 20 days of compute vs 7 hours: rethinking what state-of-the-art means — Bertrand Charpentier, Pruna
